title: edit.rs DIFF marker uses serialize-into-format-string instead of structured event
description:
## Severity
Trivial

## Dimension
Decoupling / Naming

## Location
src/tools/file/edit.rs:104-115 (DIFF_START/DIFF_END marker embedded in result string)

## Violation
```rust
let diff_json = serde_json::json!({"path": path, "old": old_string, "new": new_string});
let mut result = format!(
    "Successfully edited '{}'. Replaced {} chars with {} chars.\n@@DIFF_START@@\n{}\n@@DIFF_END@@",
    path, old_string.len(), new_string.len(), diff_json,
);
```

This couples the tool to an out-of-band display protocol via in-band string markers. The downstream renderer must scan EVERY tool result for `@@DIFF_START@@` and `@@DIFF_END@@`, parse the JSON between them, and route to a color renderer. This is precisely the architectural anti-pattern Slack calls "stringly-typed events" — semantic data embedded as substrings inside a free-form text payload. The `(String, bool)` return type forces this hack (see broader issue #670 — Result-typed returns would carry structured diff data alongside the message).

If a model ever generates the literal string "@@DIFF_START@@" inside `new_string` (entirely possible in code comments or test fixtures), it breaks the renderer's parser and may inject arbitrary JSON into the diff pane.

## Mandated refactor
After the Result<ToolOutput, ToolError> refactor (issue #670), make ToolOutput carry an optional `diff: Option<DiffData>` field. The dispatcher renders the diff via its own type-safe code path. Delete the @@DIFF_START@@ markers entirely. Until then: at minimum, base64-encode or sentinel-escape the JSON payload between markers.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
